summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Correlation (r)'?

"Correlation (r)" means: Measures the strength and direction of a linear relationship between two quantitative variables; -1 ≤ r ≤ +1. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Causation'?

"Causation" means: A change in x directly produces a change in y; requires ruling out confounding variables and establishing a mechanism. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Confounding in regression'?

"Confounding in regression" means: A third variable associated with both x and y that creates a spurious apparent relationship between them. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Establishing causation'?

"Establishing causation" means: Requires: (1) association, (2) temporal precedence (cause before effect), (3) elimination of confounders (typically through random assignment in experiments). The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Lurking variable'?

In Correlation and Causation, "Lurking variable" refers to: An unobserved variable that influences both the explanatory and response variables, creating a false impression of a direct relationship.
